notes: Quercetin: dietary baseline ~20 uM (in-vitro ref). Cytokines: healthy adult serum medians. Barrier integrity=1 (TEER reference). PMID 23339932 (Wine et al. 2013)

Quercetin ~45 uM: low-grade mucosal inflammation range from in-vitro UC models (Jiang et al. 2024 Front Pharmacol). Cytokines: mild UC reference ranges from literature. Colonization ~78% per FMT analogy studies.
Quercetin ~90 uM: moderate DSS-colitis equivalent (Zhao et al. 2024 J Mater Chem B). Cytokines elevated 3-5x baseline. Barrier ~0.62 (TEER reduction in active UC). Colonization ~65%.

Quercetin ~160 uM: severe colitis equivalent (Bagheri et al. 2026 Eur J Pharmacol; acetic acid model). IL-6 risk marker for steroid failure (Wine et al. 2013 PMID 23339932). Colonization ~42% (FMT severe IBD engraftment data).
Crohn's mild: quercetin slightly lower than UC mild due to discontinuous inflammation. Cytokine profile Th1-skewed vs UC Th2. Permeability elevated early (lactulose/mannitol ratio >0.05 in mild CD).
Crohn's moderate: significant transmural inflammation. L/M ratio 0.10-0.20 in active CD (Peeters et al. BMJ 2000 reference). Dysbiosis score high: Bifidobacterium depleted in active Crohn's.

Crohn's severe: near-complete barrier failure, very high permeability. Colonization efficiency low (~35%): recipient ecological resistance is high in severe dysbiosis (Eiseman et al. FMT analogy; Terveer et al. 2021 Nat Rev Gastroenterol).
